mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in INDPRO indicates expanding industrial output that lifts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day returns following positive INDPRO momentum do not exceed random baseline with t-test p > 0.05, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: UNRATE falling below 4.0 percent signals a tight labor market that drives wage-cost fears and reduces profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months when UNRATE is below 4.0%, or Rank IC > 0.0 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS and PAYEMS indicate a monetary tightening cycle without concurrent labor market weakness, and this combination generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as discount rates reprice higher.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month AND PAYEMS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal does not outperform the sum of individual univariate signals by at least 2 percentage points in hit rate, or interaction term t-statistic falls below 1.5 in OOS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 during late-cycle expansions generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because output growth above potential reinforces earnings expectations before monetary tightening fully transmits.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises in late-cycle quarters are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in RSAFS below its trailing 12-month average signals weakening consumer spending momentum that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns following RSAFS drops below its 12-month average are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > 0 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during monetary tightening cycles because higher policy rates immediately reprice short-term equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns follow rising FEDFUNDS in more than 52% of observations during tightening cycles, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: INDPRO falling more than 1 percent over three months signals a manufacturing contraction that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO 3-month declines exceeding 1% are followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or Rank IC > 0 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in HOUST and decreases in UNRATE signal an overheating construction labor market that tightens input costs and compresses housing margin expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases month-over-month while UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint condition does not generate negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample regression.
